request.question:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin; reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: doxorubicin folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles HeLa cells drug accumulation
hypothesis.original_hypothesis:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

references.title: Selective targeting and degradation of doxorubicin-loaded folate-functionalized DNA nanocages.
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses folate-functionalized nanocarriers for doxorubicin delivery, which is directly relevant to the hypothesis about folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles.

references.title: Indocyanine green/doxorubicin-encapsulated functionalized nanoparticles for effective combination therapy against human MDR breast cancer.
references.relevance_note: This study involves doxorubicin-encapsulated nanoparticles and their application to cancer treatment, though in a different biological system, showing the relevance of the drug delivery methodology.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention involves conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles, which is similar to the approaches described in paper [3] that focuses on folate-functionalized DNA nanocages for doxorubicin. There are also papers discussing doxorubicin-loaded functionalized nanoparticles applied to drug delivery systems, such as paper [4], which, while targeting a different biological system (MDR breast cancer), utilizes a similar method of functionalization. Thus, while not an exact match, the general methodology and focus on nanocarriers for drug delivery to cancer cells connect the hypothesis to existing literature.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To determine the intracellular drug accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment involves synthesizing folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles for doxorubicin loading and then evaluating the drug's accumulation in HeLa cells. A comparison will be made with free doxorubicin to assess the targeted delivery and cytotoxicity in both folate receptor-positive HeLa cells and folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells.
controls: Free doxorubicin at a concentration of 1 µM.
Unloaded PLGA nanoparticles without doxorubicin in HeLa cells.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Dissolve 100 mg of PLGA (e.g. Resomer RG 503H, Evonik) in 5 mL of DCM at room temperature to create a 20% w/v solution.
Expected outcome: Complete dissolution of PLGA.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Reagent Preparation
Dissolve 10 mg of folic acid in 1 mL of DMSO to form a 10 mM folic acid solution.
Expected outcome: Homogeneous folic acid solution.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Add 1 mL of the 10 mM folic acid solution to the 20% w/v PLGA solution and stir for 1 h at room temperature to allow covalent binding of folic acid to PLGA.
Expected outcome: Complete binding of folate to PLGA.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Add 1 mL of doxorubicin (1 mM in DMSO) to the folate-functionalized PLGA solution and sonicate for 10 min (30 s on, 30 s off) using a probe sonicator (e.g. 30% power, 20 kHz) for drug loading.
Expected outcome: Uniform drug-loaded nanoparticles.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Nanoparticle Characterization
Centrifuge the doxorubicin-loaded nanoparticles at 10,000 × g for 30 min at 4°C to separate unbound doxorubicin.
Expected outcome: Pelleted nanoparticles with unbound doxorubicin in the supernatant.

DAY 1 | 24 h | Cell Culture
Seed 1 x 10^5 HeLa cells in 2 mL of DMEM (supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin) in a 6-well plate and incubate at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 h to allow adherence.
Expected outcome: Adherent HeLa cell monolayer.

DAY 2 | 4 h | Drug Treatment
Treat HeLa cells with either 1 µM free doxorubicin or equivalent concentration of doxorubicin-loaded nanoparticles (determined from drug loading efficiency) in 2 mL of fresh media for 4 h at 37°C.
Expected outcome: Cells treated with doxorubicin formulations.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Analysis of Drug Accumulation
Lyse cells with 0.5 mL of RIPA buffer on ice for 15 min and quantify doxorubicin accumulation using fluorescence spectrophotometry (e.g. excitation at 485 nm and emission at 590 nm).
Expected outcome: Fluorescence readings indicating doxorubicin accumulation in cells.

DAY 2 | 15 min | Statistical Analysis
Apply a two-tailed t-test to compare fluorescence measurements of HeLa cells treated with doxorubicin-loaded nanoparticles versus those treated with free doxorubicin.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance established if p < 0.05.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Folate-functionalization increases nanoparticle uptake in HeLa cells.
Doxorubicin retains its cytotoxicity when conjugated to PLGA nanoparticles.
Cell viability assays accurately reflect intracellular drug accumulation.
decision_criteria: Proceed if doxorubicin accumulation in HeLa cells is at least 3-fold higher compared to free doxorubicin.
Verify reduction in cytotoxicity of nanoparticles by at least 40% in HEK293 cells.
Confirm successful synthesis through characterization methods such as dynamic light scattering.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Standardize PLGA batch number for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number for HeLa and HEK293 cells.
Calibrate the fluorescence spectrophotometer before each experimental run.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin; reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40% improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the intracellular drug accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
